response.justification: The candidate is technically impressive — strong TypeScript, React, AWS, Kubernetes, and even LLM/MCP work that hits nearly every required and nice-to-have checkbox. However, this is a junior/mid-level entry-level role, and the candidate brings 15+ years of experience across Staff and Senior-level positions at multiple companies. The overqualification gap is the dominant concern: retention risk is high, salary expectations will almost certainly exceed the role's band, and an entry-level IC scope is unlikely to engage someone who has been architecting platforms and mentoring teams for over a decade. The 'maybe' reflects that the technical fit is near-perfect and the Berlin location aligns, so if the candidate has a specific personal reason for pursuing a step-down role (lifestyle, industry pivot into renewables/climate), a short screening call to probe motivation is worth the time.
strengths: Deep TypeScript and Node.js expertise across multiple production-scale roles, directly matching the core stack requirement
Hands-on experience with React, AWS (CDK/ECS-adjacent), and Kubernetes, covering the majority of nice-to-have qualifications
Recent AI-agent and MCP tooling work at re:cinq demonstrates current LLM integration skills, a differentiating nice-to-have
concerns: Severe overqualification — 15+ years of Staff/Senior-level experience far exceeds the entry-level scope; retention and engagement risk is very high
No explicit PostgreSQL experience mentioned; database background skews toward MongoDB and MySQL, which may require ramp-up on a PostgreSQL-centric stack

No NestJS experience listed, and the candidate's framework history (Ember, GraphQL, custom Node servers) suggests they may have strong opinions that conflict with adopting the team's existing conventions

key_factors.factor: Core stack alignment (TypeScript, React, AWS, Kubernetes)
